raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Direct Marketing Program operated by the California Department of Food and Agriculture (CDFA) empowers farmers to sell their produce directly to customers at Certified Farmers’ Markets without adhering to the same packing and labeling requirements as grocery store products.",
    "The CDFA’s Direct Marketing Program has been in operation since 1977.",
    "The success of the Direct Marketing Program over forty years indicates its integral role in California's agricultural community.",
    "The Direct Marketing Program fosters consumer confidence in agricultural products purchased at certified farmers’ markets, community supported agriculture organizations, and local farm stands.",
    "The oversight provided by the Direct Marketing Program prevents the sale of agricultural products sourced from outside the state or from wholesale stores under the guise of being 'locally-grown.'",
    "California has approximately 700 Certified Farmers’ Markets and 2,200 certified producers."
  ]
}
```
